name: The Reciprocity Fund
notes: Active fund in directory. Listed on T25 2023.

review_priority.rationale: Met directory criteria. Indigenous-led agriculture and agroforestry cooperatives in Latin America with explicit non-extractive philosophy and Indigenous community governance.

name: FINAPOP
notes: Active fund in directory. Listed on T25 2024.

review_priority.rationale: Met directory criteria. Created explicitly to finance cooperatives transitioning to agroecology in Brazil's agrarian reform settlements. Any Brazilian citizen can invest directly.

name: NESsT Lirio Fund
notes: Active fund in directory. NESsT listed on IA50 2026, T25 2021, and CSAF as regional affiliate.

review_priority.rationale: Met directory criteria. Impact lending to smallholder farmer enterprises in Latin America with explicit food systems and dignified livelihoods mandate.

name: Barka Fund
notes: Active fund in directory. Listed on T25 2023.

review_priority.rationale: Met directory criteria. West Africa cooperative lending fund with explicit agroecology and food sovereignty alignment.

name: Pachamama Coffee Cooperative
notes: Active fund in directory. Not found in any of the five scanned source lists — absence is itself notable data.

review_priority.rationale: Met directory criteria. Farmer-owned cooperative financing model with direct agroecology alignment. Notably absent from all five scanned source lists.

name: Neycha Accelerator & Fund
notes: Active fund in directory. Listed on T25 2024, Biovision network, and assessed in TIFS 2023 report with 100% SIA alignment score.

review_priority.rationale: Met directory criteria. East Africa's first private sector fund exclusively focused on agroecology. Scored 100% on Environment and Life & Biodiversity in TIFS SIA assessment.

name: BTG Pactual Timberland Investment Group LLC
Cooperative Fund of the Northeast Inc

review_priority.rationale: Farmland access fund for regenerative farmers in the US using impact-first, non-extractive terms. Closest analog in the list to the directory's lending model on land tenure.

name: East Bay Permanent Real Estate Cooperative

review_priority.rationale: 20+ years investing in nature-based and food businesses in Latin America with a conservation co-mandate. Closer to explicitly aligned than adjacent; long track record.

Organic and regenerative farmland financing via long-term leases and mortgages. Explicit organic/regenerative mandate with farmer-first governance. Fills a US farmland gap absent from the current six.
Private credit specifically for mid-sized US farms transitioning to regenerative organic production. Very focused mandate with no diversification into other sectors.

name: Omnivore Capital Management Advisors Private Limited
Quadria Capital Investment Management Pte Ltd
UNICEF USA Impact Fund for Children
Vox Capital Gestão de Recursos LTda
Working Capital for Community Needs Inc
Affiliated Tribes of NW Indians Financial Services

review_priority.rationale: Food sovereignty framing and Global South women-led mandate. Primarily grant-based which may fall outside the directory's loan/equity criteria — needs verification.

name: Friends of the Public Bank East Bay
The Fund for Jobs Worth Owning
Infinity Point CDFI & Local Ownership Catalyst Fund
New Majority Capital Fund I LP
Right to the City Integrated Loan Fund
Alterfin
notes: CSAF Global Member

notes: CSAF Global Member

name: Rabo Rural Fund
notes: CSAF Global Member

name: Shared Interest
notes: CSAF Global Member

review_priority.rationale: Guarantee fund for fair trade producer organizations in Africa and Latin America. Cooperative-first, smallholder-direct capital mechanism with strong overlap with directory criteria.

name: Triodos Investment Management
notes: CSAF Global Member

notes: CSAF Global Affiliate

name: CFC / Common Fund for Commodities
notes: CSAF Global Affiliate

notes: CSAF Global Affiliate

notes: CSAF Global Affiliate

name: AgDevCo
notes: CSAF Regional Affiliate

name: Ecotierra
notes: CSAF Regional Affiliate

name: FAPY — Fondo Agroecológico para la Península de Yucatán
notes: First regional fund seeded by Agroecology Fund, est. 2020. Grant-based vehicle operated by Túumben K'óoben Cooperative. Capital deployment mechanism differs from active directory funds.

review_priority.rationale: Genuinely agroecological mandate supporting Mayan food systems in Yucatán. Currently grant-based — needs verification on whether any recoverable capital is deployed.

name: Bharat Agroecology Fund (BAF)
notes: India regional fund est. 2022, hosted by Bharathiya Vikas Trust. Targets 20M smallholders transitioning to agroecology by 2030. Primarily philanthropic/grant capital.

name: Regional Agroecology Fund — West Africa
notes: Incubation stage. Seeded by Agroecology Fund, hosted by TrustAfrica. Supports grassroots agroecology networks and Indigenous/women-led orgs. Not yet a formal investment vehicle.

name: Regional Agroecology Fund — East Africa
notes: Incubation stage. Seeded by Agroecology Fund, hosted by TrustAfrica. Supports networks and Indigenous/women-led orgs. Not yet a formal investment vehicle.

name: Northern Cooperative Development Bank (NCDB)
notes: Federation of 1,200 rural cooperatives operating as a development bank in post-civil war northern Sri Lanka. Revolving loan fund lent $1.3M of $1.68M fund to cooperative members in 2023. Not a traditional investment fund; grassroots cooperative credit model.

name: CNOP-CAM Revolving Fund
notes: Revolving fund operated by Concertation Nationale des Organisations Paysannes au Cameroun (CNOP-CAM). Low-interest loans to women farmers practicing agroecology. Supported by Agroecology Fund grantmaking. Grassroots credit model, not a formal investment vehicle.

name: GRABE Benin Community Savings Fund (Caisses Communautaires)
notes: Self-managed community savings system for rural women farmers in Benin, operated by GRABE Benin. Weekly pooling of resources with low-interest loans for farm enterprises. Not a formal investment fund.

review_priority.rationale: Agroforestry equity fund operating in Latin America and sub-Saharan Africa. Agroforestry is one of the 13 HLPE agroecology principles and the fund's exclusive mandate.
Explicitly includes agroecology in its investment mandate; East Africa focus. Small and relatively new but directly relevant. Featured in TIFS report and Biovision's investment guide.

name: Africa Agriculture and Trade Investment Fund (AATIF)
Social Enterprise Fund for Agriculture in Africa
